title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
prompt:
Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values.

Level order traversal visits nodes level by level from left to right. Return an array of arrays, where each inner array contains the values for that level.
If the tree is empty, return an empty array.
constraintQuestions: What should we return for an empty tree? ([]).
Do we need to preserve left-to-right order within each level? (Yes.)
Should we use breadth-first search with a queue?
Do we care about node values beyond collecting them? (No.)
Is the output an array of arrays? (Yes.)

walkthrough.title: BFS with queue
walkthrough.body:
Use a queue to process nodes level by level.
For each level, capture the current queue size and process exactly that many nodes.
Collect their values into a level array and push it into the result.

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(n) because each node is visited once.
Space: O(n) for the queue and output.
